pending.body:
finding_summary: The newly added benchmark instructions for `ralph-burning-juj` will not run the release profile as written. The command places `--release` after `--`, so Cargo forwards it to the test binary instead of enabling release-mode compilation, which invalidates the bead's step-1 hypothesis check.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- .beads/issues.jsonl
evidence:
- .beads/issues.jsonl:117 embeds `cargo test --locked --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_start_releases_lock_on_error -- --exact --release`.
- `cargo test --help` documents `--release` as a Cargo compilation option that must appear before the `-- [ARGS]...` separator.
remediation_hint: Move `--release` into Cargo's option section, for example `cargo test --release --locked --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_start_releases_lock_on_error -- --exact`, and make the same fix anywhere else this measurement command is copied.

The acceptance criteria specify 'Target: single CLI lifecycle test under 15 seconds (currently 67-85s)' and 'Measurable reduction in stub-backend test execution time'. Neither criterion is met. While the hypothesis was correctly tested and disproven (release mode yields ~6% improvement on the single test but the full suite and conformance are actually slower), the performance problem persists at 69.65s — 4.6x over the 15s target. A new bead should investigate the actual bottleneck. The release-vs-debug data strongly suggests the overhead is not CPU-bound (compiler optimizations don't help), so the root cause is likely: (1) excessive sleep/timeout durations in stub backends, (2) subprocess spawning overhead, or (3) synchronization/polling intervals in the test harness. Profiling with strace/perf to identify the dominant wait source would be the right next step.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
